author: Augustine of Hippo
book: A Work on the Proceedings of Pelagius.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: A Work on the Proceedings of Pelagius.
The Answer of the Monk Pelagius and His Profession of Faith.
title: The Answer of the Monk Pelagius and His Profession of Faith.

verses.book: A Work on the Proceedings of Pelagius.
verses.text: Chapter 43 [XIX.]—The Answer of the Monk Pelagius and His Profession of Faith.

verses.book: A Work on the Proceedings of Pelagius.
verses.text: After all these sentences were read out, the synod said: “What says the monk Pelagius to all these heads of opinion which have been read in his presence?

verses.book: A Work on the Proceedings of Pelagius.
verses.text: For this holy synod condemns the whole, as does also God’s Holy Catholic Church.”

verses.book: A Work on the Proceedings of Pelagius.
verses.text: Pelagius answered: “I say again, that these opinions, even according to their own testimony, are not mine; nor for them, as I have already said, ought I to be held responsible.

verses.book: A Work on the Proceedings of Pelagius.
verses.text: The opinions which I have confessed to be my own, I maintain are sound; those, however, which I have said are not my own, I reject according to the judgment of this holy synod, pronouncing anathema on every man who opposes and gainsays the doctrines of the Holy Catholic Church.

verses.book: A Work on the Proceedings of Pelagius.
verses.text: For I believe in the Trinity of the one substance, and I hold all things in accordance with the teaching of the Holy Catholic Church.

verses.book: A Work on the Proceedings of Pelagius.
verses.text: If indeed any man entertains opinions different from her, let him be anathema.”
